meaning: vt.具体表达,使具体化,包含,收录
examples: As a ballerina, to embody the duality of the Swan Queen and the black swan can be a fiendishly difficult task.
Indeed, the streets of San Jose seem, in some ways, to embody the best of America.
British cars, once memorably described as a skip on wheels, have come to embody the national humiliation of that benighted era.
Military and business leaders that embody this principal empower their team and earn respect.
Just listen to Dominique de Villepin, the prime minister, who came to embody anti-American defiance.
